<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file** • Edit file: /Users/pabloaguilar/Dropbox/code/monopolytracker/src/components/atoms/Toggle.tsx</summary>

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,28 @@
- 
+ // #index
+ // - //#component: accessible toggle switch (green when on)
+ 
+ import React from 'react';
+ 
+ export type ToggleProps = {
+   checked: boolean;
+   onChange: (next: boolean) =&gt; void;
+   qa?: string;
+ };
+ 
+ export default function Toggle({ checked, onChange, qa }: ToggleProps): JSX.Element {
+   return (
+     &lt;button
+       type="button"
+       role="switch"
+       aria-checked={checked}
+       data-qa={qa}
+       onClick={() =&gt; onChange(!checked)}
+       className={&#96;${checked ? 'bg-teal-500' : 'bg-neutral-400'} relative inline-flex h-6 w-10 items-center rounded-full transition-colors focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-teal-300&#96;}
+       title={checked ? 'On' : 'Off'}
+     &gt;
+       &lt;span
+         className={&#96;${checked ? 'translate-x-4' : 'translate-x-0'} inline-block h-5 w-5 transform rounded-full bg-white transition-transform&#96;}
+       /&gt;
+     &lt;/button&gt;
+   );
+ }
```

</details>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Planning Race Pot Implementation**

I need to implement the Start Game deduction for the Race Pot and badge on the Play page since the user said "go." I’ll consider creating a race pot state, possibly in the session slice or a new pot slice. When starting the game, I should check if the number of opted-in players is two or more. If so, I'll compute the pot and deduct from their money, while also creating a badge UI to show the pot amount and participants. I might also want to track if the pot is active and who the winner is.
**Defining Session State**

I’m defining the SessionState in the session slice to include the current game ID and race pot information. I’ll add actions like initializeRacePot, which will set up the race pot at the game start, and awardRacePot for later use. I’ll need to deduct money from opted-in players only after confirming the game start. In SetupForm’s onStart, if there are two or more participants, I’ll adjust their money and initialize the race pot. Lastly, I’ll ensure the Play page displays the pot amount and participant count using the session data.
**Implementing Race Pot Actions**

I’m adding a new type and actions to extend the session slice to include the race pot. The racePot state will track whether it's active, the total amount, participants, winner ID, and whether it has been awarded. I’ll set up the reducers to initialize, award, and reset the race pot even if we only need to use initialize for now. In SetupForm’s onStart, I’ll derive participants and if there are two or more, I’ll dispatch the initialization and adjust player money. I also need to consider appending events for money adjustment and pot creation.
**Adding Event and UI for Race Pot**

I can simply append a MONEY_ADJUST event for each participant and maybe add a FEE or MISC event as well. The earlier prompt includes EventType union, and the code uses MONEY_ADJUST. I’ll create an event with a payload like "Race pot buy-in -100." Additionally, it’s necessary to display a badge on the PlayConsole using session.racePot and players. I’ll compute the display to show something like "🏃‍➡️💰 $<amount> (n participants)" at the top-right of PlayConsole. I'll make sure the Race Pot deduction only happens at the start to avoid duplication when re-entering Setup.
**Implementing Setup and Race Pot Initialization**

I need to ensure that when the user clicks the "Start Game" button, we navigate to /play responsibly. There’s a risk of double deducting money if they click multiple times, so I’ll disable the button after the first click. I’ll also check if session.racePot.active is true before deducing money to prevent re-initialization. In SetupForm, I’ll use useSelector to access this state and proceed only if there are enough participants. I’ll import necessary types and actions into sessionSlice, and add the pot badge UI in PlayConsole. It might be good to append events related to money adjustments as well.</details></think>

  import { createSlice, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';
+ 
+ type RacePotState = {
+   active: boolean;
+   amount: number;
+   participants: string[]; // player ids
+   winnerId: string | null;
+ };
  
  type SessionState = {
    currentGameId: string | null;
+   racePot: RacePotState;
  };
  
  const initialState: SessionState = {
    currentGameId: null,

        state.currentGameId = action.payload;
      },
+     initializeRacePot(state, action: PayloadAction&lt;{ participants: string[]; amountPerPlayer: number }&gt;) {
+       const { participants, amountPerPlayer } = action.payload;
+       if (participants.length &lt; 2 || state.racePot.active) return;
+       state.racePot.active = true;
+       state.racePot.participants = participants;
+       state.racePot.amount = participants.length * amountPerPlayer;
+       state.racePot.winnerId = null;
+     },
+     resetRacePot(state) {
+       state.racePot = { active: false, amount: 0, participants: [], winnerId: null };
+     },
    },
  });
  
- export const { setCurrentGameId } = sessionSlice.actions;

    const onStart = (): void =&gt; {
      if (SHOW_CARD_SEED) dispatch(setSeed(seed));

+     if (participants.length &gt;= 2) {

+       for (const pid of participants) {

+       }
+     }
      navigate('/play');
    };

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

Here’s the tight next-up list. Pick one and say “go”:

- Tile-aware prompts after Apply Move: auto-suggest Pay Rent/Tax/Draw Card/Go To Jail with quick-action buttons.
- Race pot auto-award: on first qualifying pass of GO (including teleport/advance), award pot, lock pot, log event, badge shows winner.
- Card resolution UI: when a card is drawn, show its text with a “Resolve” action that logs effects.
- Undo last action (scoped): support undo for money adjustments, rent, tax, and property purchase.
- Cloud share (Supabase) foundation: anon auth, game table, RLS, simple “share code” link, realtime spectator sync.

- We just shipped race pot init/deduction + Play badge, and made the toggle reusable and clickable via the emoji.

      },
+     setTurnIndex(state, action: PayloadAction&lt;number&gt;) {
+       state.turnIndex = Math.max(0, action.payload);
+     },
+     advanceTurn(state, action: PayloadAction&lt;{ playerCount: number }&gt;) {
+       const n = action.payload.playerCount;
+       if (n &lt;= 0) {
+         state.turnIndex = 0;
+       } else {
+         state.turnIndex = (state.turnIndex + 1) % n;

+         {players.map((p, idx) =&gt; {
+           const isActive = idx === turnIndex;
+           return (
+             &lt;div key={p.id} className={&#96;rounded-lg border ${isActive ? 'border-emerald-400' : 'border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700'} p-3 bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900&#96;}&gt; 
+               &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-3"&gt;
+                 &lt;div style={{ ['--player-color' as any]: p.color } as React.CSSProperties}&gt;
+                   &lt;AvatarToken emoji={'🙂'} borderColorClass="border-[color:var(--player-color)]" ring ringColorClass="ring-[color:var(--player-color)]" size={36} /&gt;
+                 &lt;/div&gt;
+                 &lt;div className="font-semibold"&gt;{idx + 1}. {p.nickname}&lt;/div&gt;
+                 &lt;div className="ml-auto text-sm opacity-80"&gt;${p.money}&lt;/div&gt;
+               &lt;/div&gt;
+               {isActive &amp;&amp; (
+                 &lt;div className="mt-3 space-y-3"&gt;
+                   &lt;div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-3"&gt;
+                     &lt;div&gt;
+                       &lt;div className="text-xs font-medium mb-1"&gt;Pre-actions&lt;/div&gt;
+                       &lt;select className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-2 py-1 text-sm dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:border-neutral-700"&gt;
+                         &lt;option&gt;None&lt;/option&gt;
+                         &lt;option&gt;Mortgage&lt;/option&gt;
+                         &lt;option&gt;Unmortgage&lt;/option&gt;
+                         &lt;option&gt;Build&lt;/option&gt;
+                         &lt;option&gt;Sell&lt;/option&gt;
+                         &lt;option&gt;Money Adjust&lt;/option&gt;
+                       &lt;/select&gt;
+                     &lt;/div&gt;
+                     &lt;div&gt;
+                       &lt;div className="text-xs font-medium mb-1"&gt;Dice&lt;/div&gt;
+                       &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-2"&gt;
+                         &lt;input type="number" min={1} max={6} value={d6A} onChange={(e) =&gt; setD6A(Math.max(1, Math.min(6, parseInt(e.target.value || '1', 10))))} className="w-16 rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-2 py-1 text-sm dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:border-neutral-700" /&gt;
+                         &lt;input type="number" min={1} max={6} value={d6B} onChange={(e) =&gt; setD6B(Math.max(1, Math.min(6, parseInt(e.target.value || '1', 10))))} className="w-16 rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-2 py-1 text-sm dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:border-neutral-700" /&gt;
+                         &lt;select value={String(special)} onChange={(e) =&gt; setSpecial((isNaN(Number(e.target.value)) ? (e.target.value as SpecialDieFace) : (Number(e.target.value) as SpecialDieFace)))} className="rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-2 py-1 text-sm dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:border-neutral-700"&gt;
+                           &lt;option value="1"&gt;1&lt;/option&gt;
+                           &lt;option value="2"&gt;2&lt;/option&gt;
+                           &lt;option value="3"&gt;3&lt;/option&gt;
+                           &lt;option value="+1"&gt;+1&lt;/option&gt;
+                           &lt;option value="-1"&gt;-1&lt;/option&gt;
+                           &lt;option value="Bus"&gt;Bus&lt;/option&gt;
+                         &lt;/select&gt;
+                       &lt;/div&gt;
+                     &lt;/div&gt;

+                       Update Turn

- Added HUD on Play (houses, hotels, bank-owned count, race pot badge).
- Added per-player turn cards in play order; the active player shows pre-actions, dice inputs, post-actions, and “Update Turn” (dice required).

_**Agent (gpt-5 2025-08-12 11:07:32Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Implementing wizard-like stepper**

I need to implement a wizard-like stepper for the active player card using Framer Motion pages that show one step at a time, incorporating a ghost-style "Next" button and an "End Turn" button requiring a 3-second hold to confirm. Currently, PlayConsole doesn't import framer-motion, so I need to modify PlayConsole.tsx to include AnimatePresence and motion. The sequence should be Pre-actions → Roll → Post-actions, with specific flow requirements for transitioning between each step.
**Implementing End Turn button**

I need to set up a 3-second hold on the "End Turn" button for confirmation. To do this, I'll create a state for tracking hold progress. When the button is pressed, a timer will start counting down, updating the progress. If the button is released, I'll cancel the timer. When the time completes, this will trigger the end turn process, applying movement, advancing turns, and resetting the step. The End Turn should call onApplyMove for the active player’s ID while maintaining a ghost style using Tailwind CSS classes for design.
**Integrating Framer Motion**

I need to integrate Framer Motion by importing AnimatePresence and motion. I’ll set up a step index to track the active step, resetting it to 0 for turn changes and ensuring that "Roll" shows the total and a confirmation option. The "Next" button from Roll to Post actions should be disabled until both hasRoll and rollConfirmed are true. For the "End Turn" function, I'll implement hold mechanics using useRef for timeouts and intervals, tracking hold progress. I'll also adjust the PlayConsole.tsx without using the apply_patch tool, while being cautious with the Tailwind classes.
**Implementing Framer Motion setup**

I’ll start by importing AnimatePresence and motion from 'framer-motion.' Next, I need to establish state variables for activeStep (set to 0), holdProgress (also 0), and a holding flag set to false. When the turnIndex changes, I'll ensure activeStep resets to 0 and clear the hold progress. For the UI, I’ll use AnimatePresence to wrap sections based on the activeStep, ensuring only one section is displayed at a time. The button area will include a ghost-style "Next" button for steps 0 and 1, and a primary "End Turn" button for step 2.
**Finalizing End Turn functionality**

I’ll ensure that the "Next" button from Roll (1) to Post (2) remains disabled until both hasRoll and rollConfirmed are satisfied. For the "End Turn" hold feature, I'll implement handlers to start holding, set holding to true, and schedule an interval to increment holdProgress to 100 over 3000 ms. Once it reaches 100, I'll call endTurn, which will trigger onApplyMove(p.id) and reset holdProgress. I'll also remove the "Update Turn" button to avoid duplication, since the "End Turn" button incorporates movement actions. Additionally, I need to ensure tile-aware suggestions are consistent with TypeScript types.</details></think>

+   const onEndTurnHoldStart = (pid: string): void =&gt; {
+     if (!hasRoll || !rollConfirmed) return;
+     setHoldProgress(0);
+     const start = Date.now();
+     const duration = 3000;
+     holdIntervalRef.current = window.setInterval(() =&gt; {
+       const elapsed = Date.now() - start;
+       const progress = Math.min(100, Math.round((elapsed / duration) * 100));
+       setHoldProgress(progress);
+     }, 50) as unknown as number;
+     holdTimerRef.current = window.setTimeout(() =&gt; {
+       // Confirmed
+       if (holdIntervalRef.current) window.clearInterval(holdIntervalRef.current);
+       setHoldProgress(100);
+       onApplyMove(pid);
+     }, duration) as unknown as number;
+   };
+

+     if (holdTimerRef.current) window.clearTimeout(holdTimerRef.current);

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Designing a tracker UI**

I need to implement a stepper or tracker UI above paged sections in PlayConsole. The design includes an indicator with three steps: Pre Action, Roll, and Post Action, each with a date and helpful subtitles. I'll make these sections clickable for navigation. I'll maintain a summary state object that updates with current actions, ensuring that pre-actions, roll summaries, and post-actions adapt as selections are made. Currently, we have the necessary selects, but I need to store their values.
**Implementing stepper UI**

I want to add a stepper UI above the AnimatePresence area with three items. Each step should have an icon, like a check for completed and a ring for the active step. They need to be clickable to set the activeStep based on the highestCompleted status. I’ll track the highestVisitedStep and define completed steps. For Step 0, it’s always accessible; it’s completed once the user clicks Next to Step 1. The function getStepStatus will help in managing the steps and their statuses. I'll ensure proper conditions for navigation.
**Enhancing the stepper UI**

In the stepper, I want to allow users to click on completed or current steps, but limit access to upcoming ones unless certain conditions are met. Each step will have a brief description underneath. For Pre-actions, it could show preAction or "None," while the Roll summary displays a formula format. I’ll design the CSS to match the screenshot, featuring a progress bar and circles with checks for completed steps. Instead of dates, I'll label the steps as Pre, Roll, and Post with descriptions below. I'll set up the container and background line properly!
**Streamlining the stepper design**

I’m simplifying the stepper design by using straightforward boxes for each step. Each will include a label at the top, a circle with a checkmark for completed steps or a ring for the current one, and a description below. I’ll stack these vertically in three columns within the active card area of PlayConsole. I need to track state values for preAction and postAction, as well as the highestStep variable. The Next button will control the active step flow, updating the highestStep accordingly. I'll also add a helper function for the roll summary string!
**Constructing the stepper UI**

I’m focusing on implementing the stepper UI by creating an array to hold the step information. This includes titles and descriptions for each step: Pre Action, Roll, and Post Action. Each step should be clickable based on the highestStep or activeStep status. I’ll define their statuses: done, current, or upcoming, and use visual indicators for each. Additionally, I need to update the select inputs for preAction and postAction, ensuring handlers are added for interaction. After that, I’ll proceed to implement the modifications in PlayConsole.tsx. Let's keep moving forward!</details></think>

+                         const status = s.id &lt; activeStep ? 'done' : s.id === activeStep ? 'current' : 'upcoming';
+                         const clickable = s.id &lt;= highestStep || s.id === activeStep;
+                         return (
+                           &lt;div key={s.id} className="flex-1 flex flex-col items-center text-center"&gt;
+                             {/* top label */}
+                             &lt;div className="text-xs font-medium mb-1"&gt;{s.title}&lt;/div&gt;
+                             {/* circle */}
+                             &lt;button
+                               type="button"
+                               disabled={!clickable}
+                               onClick={() =&gt; clickable &amp;&amp; setActiveStep(s.id)}
+                               className={&#96;relative z-10 inline-flex h-8 w-8 items-center justify-center rounded-full border transition-colors ${
+                                 status === 'done'
+                                   ? 'bg-emerald-600 border-emerald-600 text-white'
+                                   : status === 'current'
+                                   ? 'bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900 border-emerald-500 text-emerald-600'
+                                   : 'bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900 border-neutral-300 dark:border-neutral-700 text-neutral-400'
+                               } ${!clickable ? 'opacity-60 cursor-not-allowed' : ''}&#96;}
+                             &gt;
+                               {status === 'done' ? '✓' : s.id + 1}
+                             &lt;/button&gt;
+                             {/* connector line */}
+                             {i &lt; stepItems.length - 1 &amp;&amp; (
+                               &lt;div className={&#96;h-1 w-full -mt-4 ${i &lt; activeStep ? 'bg-emerald-500' : 'bg-neutral-200 dark:bg-neutral-800'}&#96;} aria-hidden /&gt;
+                             )}
+                             {/* desc */}
+                             &lt;div className="text-[11px] opacity-70 mt-2 min-h-4 px-1"&gt;{s.desc}&lt;/div&gt;
+                           &lt;/div&gt;
+                         );
+                       })}
+                     &lt;/div&gt;
+                   &lt;/div&gt;
+ 
                    &lt;AnimatePresence mode="wait"&gt;
                      {activeStep === 0 &amp;&amp; (
                        &lt;motion.div key="pre" variants={pageVariants} initial="initial" animate="enter" exit="exit" className="space-y-2"&gt;
                          &lt;div className="text-xs font-medium"&gt;Pre-actions&lt;/div&gt;
                          &lt;div&gt;
-                           &lt;select className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-2 py-1 text-sm dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:border-neutral-700"&gt;
+                           &lt;select className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-2 py-1 text-sm dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:border-neutral-700" value={preAction} onChange={(e) =&gt; setPreAction(e.target.value)}&gt;
                              &lt;option&gt;None&lt;/option&gt;
                              &lt;option&gt;Mortgage&lt;/option&gt;
```

**Chunk 5**
Lines added: 1, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -435,5 +502,5 @@
                          &lt;div className="text-xs font-medium"&gt;Post-actions&lt;/div&gt;
                          &lt;div&gt;
-                           &lt;select className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-2 py-1 text-sm dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:border-neutral-700"&gt;
+                           &lt;select className="w-full rounded-md border border-neutral-300 bg-white px-2 py-1 text-sm dark:bg-neutral-900 dark:border-neutral-700" value={postAction} onChange={(e) =&gt; setPostAction(e.target.value)}&gt;

- Added a stepper tracker above the active player pages with three steps: Pre Action, Roll, Post-action.
- Each step shows a brief summary:

  - Roll: e.g., 3+4 + +1 = 8
  - Post-action: selected option
- The tracker is clickable to navigate back to completed steps; the current/next step is styled; connectors fill as you progress.
- “Next” advances pages (ghost style). Post-action unlocks only after Confirm Roll. “End Turn” still 3s hold-to-confirm.

All changes in `src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx`. Build is green.

        setHighestStep(1);

+       // Confirm roll and auto-fill post action based on predicted destination

+       const fromIndex = players.find((p) =&gt; p.id === pid)?.positionIndex ?? currentIndex;
+       const moveSteps = (d6A as number) + (d6B as number) + specialNumeric;
+       const toIndex = (fromIndex + moveSteps) % BOARD_TILES.length;
+       const t = getTileByIndex(toIndex);
+       let suggested: string = 'None';
+       if (t.type === 'property' || t.type === 'railroad' || t.type === 'utility') {
+         const owner = propsState.byTileId[t.id]?.ownerId as string | null;
+         if (owner &amp;&amp; owner !== pid) suggested = 'Pay Rent';
+       } else if (t.type === 'tax') {
+         suggested = 'Pay Tax';
+       } else if (t.type === 'chance' || t.type === 'community') {
+         suggested = 'Draw Card';
+       }
+       setPostAction(suggested);
        setActiveStep(2);
        setHighestStep(2);

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

Auto-fill wired:

- When you press Next on Roll, we confirm the roll and pre-select Post-action based on the predicted destination:
  - Owned by other: Pay Rent
  - Tax tile: Pay Tax
  - Chance/Community: Draw Card
  - Otherwise: None

You can still change it before End Turn.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Adjusting purchase logic**

I need to rethink the logic since buying is currently disabled when a property is mortgaged. The requirement seems to say that if a property is mortgaged, a player can still purchase it. However, in standard Monopoly, mortgaged property can’t be bought from the bank as it belongs to the player who mortgaged it. It looks like I could remove the mortgaged check from canBuy, allowing purchase if the owner is null, regardless of the mortgaged status.
**Clarifying purchase conditions**

They mentioned that if a property is mortgaged, a player can still purchase it, suggesting that buying should be possible if the owner is null or possibly owned by someone else. Normally, if the property is mortgaged and owned, it can't be bought. It seems they might be using the mortgaged flag for bank-owned properties. I’ll enable purchasing when the owner is null, regardless of the mortgaged state, and make necessary adjustments in the PlayConsole and any related descriptions. Let's build this!</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

Short answer: No.

- “Buy for $…”: buys the predicted destination for the active player, deducts the price, and logs PURCHASE.
- “Buy Property” (GM controls): manual override; you pick an owner, it assigns ownership for the current tile and doesn’t auto-deduct money.

Want me to:
- Hide the GM “Buy Property” when the contextual “Buy for $…” is available, or
- Make the GM button behave the same (use active player, predicted tile, and deduct price)?

On End Turn, I need to process the buy before or after advancing the turn, since it currently auto-advances. Let’s implement these changes!

I'm planning to modify the `onApplyMove` function to include a parameter called `applyPostActions: boolean`. But actually, it might be easier to call a new function `applyPostActions(pid, tileIndex)` right after updating the position, but before advancing the turn.

After setting the current index and confirming the roll, I’ll check if `activeStep` is 2. I’ll rely on the `buySelected` state and the `postAction` string here. Inside `onApplyMove`, I can reference `buySelected`, `postAction`, and `predictedTo`, making sure that `predictedTo` matches `toIndex`. Additionally, if the `postAction` is Pay Tax, Draw, or Rent, I might implement the previously existing functions like `onPayTax`. Let's get to coding this!

The request specifically mentions only the buy action, so I’ll focus solely on that.

        {/* //#event-log: chronological record of GM actions for spectators and undo later */}
        &lt;EventLog /&gt;
+ 
+       {/* Card picker overlay */}
+       &lt;AnimatePresence&gt;
+         {overlay.deck &amp;&amp; (
+           &lt;motion.div
+             key="overlay"
+             initial={{ y: '100%' }}
+             animate={{ y: 0 }}
+             exit={{ y: '100%' }}
+             transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 260, damping: 24 }}
+             className="fixed inset-x-0 bottom-0 z-50 rounded-t-2xl bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900 border-t border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700 p-4 shadow-2xl"
+           &gt;
+             &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-2"&gt;
+               &lt;div className="text-sm font-semibold"&gt;Pick a {overlay.deck === 'community' ? 'Community Chest' : 'Chance'} card&lt;/div&gt;
+               &lt;button onClick={() =&gt; setOverlay({ deck: null })} className="text-sm opacity-70 hover:opacity-100"&gt;Close&lt;/button&gt;

+                 &lt;button
+                   key={card.id}

+                     // Move chosen card to top of discard by simulating: put all cards before it to bottom, then draw
+                     const deck = overlay.deck!;
+                     const draw = cardsState.decks[deck].drawPile;
+                     const idx = draw.findIndex((c) =&gt; c.id === card.id);
+                     if (idx &gt; 0) {
+                       for (let i = 0; i &lt; idx; i++) {
+                         const id = draw[i].id;
+                         dispatch(putCardOnBottom({ deck, cardId: id }));
+                       }

+                     );

+                   }}
+                   className="text-left rounded-md border border-neutral-200 dark:border-neutral-700 p-3 hover:bg-neutral-50 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800"

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

Added a bottom sheet card picker:

- Tapping Draw Chance or Draw Community opens a Framer Motion overlay from the bottom.
- Shows all available (not yet drawn) cards in that deck; you pick the one that matches the real card.
- On pick: moves it to the top via put-to-bottom then draw, logs the CARD event, and closes the overlay.

Also:
- Removed the duplicate Buy action.
- Ghost “Buy for $X” text contrast fixed.

_**Agent (gpt-5 2025-08-12 11:07:32Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Modifying game logic**

I need to think about adjusting our logic for a special die labeled 'Bus'. When this die is selected, the movement steps should be zero. Instead of applying it automatically, we should open an overlay for the Bus card, similar to the Chance/Community cards, allowing the GM to pick among them. Our current code does have the mapping for 'Bus' set. When special is 'Bus', it's recognized as a string, so it returns zero properly. Plus, we must change the earlier card overlay code to trigger an overlay for the 'bus'.
**Adjusting roll requirements**

I need to adjust the roll requirements to allow for the 'Bus' selection. Currently, we're only enforcing numeric values, but since the bus counts as 0 steps, we need to enable its selection as well. When 'Bus' is chosen, it should allow the Next button; our current check only recognizes numbers. I’ll update this to include 'Bus', as well as +1, -1, and other specific numbers. The movement calculation already uses specialNumeric, and it looks like bus=0 should work fine, but I also need to modify the overlay to include the bus deck.
**Updating card draw logic**

In the onDrawCard function, I need to change the logic so that if the deck is 'bus', it opens the overlay for selection, similar to the Chance/Community options. The earlier code only opened overlays for those, but now I’ll include the bus. For the bus tile, we don’t have a tile type, so no action suggestion is needed there since it’s a special die. I must also ensure that the step gating allows for 'Bus', so I’ll adjust the checks to include it along with valid numbers. The button group already includes 'Bus', which is great!
**Enabling Next button for special selection**

I need to modify the logic so that the Next button is allowed even when the selected special isn’t numeric. For the Bus selection, the total should correctly reflect specialNumeric as 0. I also want to ensure that the overlay is triggered when clicking Draw Bus in GM actions, and we might consider some UI for selecting Bus from the Roll page. The request suggests that when Bus is chosen, the overlay should appear. I’ll plan to open the overlay when the special changes to 'Bus', especially after the Confirm step.
**Finalizing overlay changes**

I’ve decided that the overlay triggered by the Draw Bus button in GM actions is a solid addition, but to improve the user experience, I think it’s best to open the overlay automatically after clicking Next when the special is 'Bus'. I’ll implement this in the goNext function when the active step is 1. Additionally, I want to ensure the overlay header reads "Bus Ticket" when the deck is 'bus' and that the roll total badge correctly shows 0 for the Bus. I'll make the necessary code updates in PlayConsole.tsx and streamline the processes around canGoNext and onDrawCard. Let's get started!</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

Yes. Two easy ways:

- DevTools
  - Open DevTools → Application → Clear storage → Clear site data → refresh.

- Console (on the app page)
```js
indexedDB.deleteDatabase('localforage');
localStorage.clear();
sessionStorage.clear();
location.reload();
```

This resets players, events, and decks. After reload, use Draw Chance/Community to see the full card list in the overlay.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Implementing bus ticket feature**

I need to add a feature for when the player has more than 0 bus tickets. It involves creating a "Use Bus ticket" button below the dice options. When clicked, it should show an overlay with a visual board representation and player markers for their current positions. Players can select a tile to teleport. After selection, I need a "check button" with a cancellation option. I'll integrate this new overlay with the existing PlayConsole.tsx settings, making simple CSS adjustments for layout.
**Creating a bus ticket modal**

I realize that coding the bus ticket feature might be time-consuming, so I'm thinking a simpler approach. Maybe a modal with a scroll grid listing tiles? But the user requested a visual representation. I’ll settle on a basic 10x10 ring layout using an 11x11 CSS grid. I need to create a function for this grid, including player positions from the players array. I'll also establish an overlay data structure for managing board states and toggling overlays effectively. The button display will depend on the bus ticket status.
**Implementing bus teleport logic**

For the End Turn function, if `busTeleportToIndex` is set, I need to decrement the bus tickets and adjust movement accordingly. When the bus special is used, players should teleport to the chosen tile instead of moving based on dice results. Previously, I calculated movement steps based on the dice roll, but with the bus ticket, teleporting allows more freedom. In `onEndTurnHoldStart`, if `busTeleportToIndex` is not null, we'll move directly to that index instead. Plus, I'll ensure that the "Use Bus ticket" option remains independent for selecting tiles during the Roll step.
**Logging bus ticket usage**

I need to decrement `busTickets` for the active player and log the event as "BUS_PASS_USED" in the event type system we discussed earlier. However, I realize that we haven't implemented this in the events slice yet. It looks like I could log it under 'CARD' or 'MONEY_ADJUST'. Since the `GameEvent` type union includes various event types, I'll safely log 'CARD' with the message "Bus ticket used to teleport." I'll also create a new reducer called `consumeBusTicket` in the players slice to manage this decrementing.
**Setting up bus ticket UI overlay**

I'm planning to implement a centered modal with a 10x10 ring grid. I’ll compute tile positions and map them to 11x11 grid coordinates. Each tile will have clickable button squares showing their names, and I'll highlight the currently selected tile with colored dots for player positions. The "Use Bus ticket" option will appear only when the active player has bus tickets. I'll need to adjust the `onEndTurnHoldStart` function to set `toIndex` to `busTeleportToIndex` if it's not null. For teleportation, I want to ensure we address the rules around passing GO. I'll create a `consumeBusTicket` reducer to manage ticket usage. Finally, I'll implement the `renderBoardOverlay()` function to flesh out the UI.</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

- Added Use Bus Ticket flow:
  - Shows when the active player has at least 1 `busTickets`.
  - Opens a centered board picker; GO at top-right, all tiles shown with indices, current tile highlighted.
  - Selecting a tile consumes 1 ticket, sets a pending teleport destination, and shows a pill: “✓ 🚐 to {Tile}”.
  - Tapping the pill cancels and restores the Use Bus Ticket button.

- Teleport destination sets `predictedTo`; End Turn will use that index for movement.

+   function getTileHeaderBg(tile: ReturnType&lt;typeof getTileByIndex&gt;): string {
+     if (tile.type === 'property') {
+       switch (tile.group) {
+         case 'brown':
+           return 'bg-amber-800';
+         case 'lightBlue':
+           return 'bg-sky-300';
+         case 'pink':
+           return 'bg-pink-400';
+         case 'orange':
+           return 'bg-orange-400';
+         case 'red':
+           return 'bg-red-600';
+         case 'yellow':
+           return 'bg-yellow-300';
+         case 'green':
+           return 'bg-green-600';
+         case 'darkBlue':
+           return 'bg-blue-900';
+       }
+     }
+     if (tile.type === 'railroad') return 'bg-stone-300';
+     if (tile.type === 'utility') return 'bg-zinc-200';
+     return 'bg-neutral-200';
+   }
+ 
+   const tileFooterIcon = (tileId: string): JSX.Element | null =&gt; {
+     const t = BOARD_TILES.find((x) =&gt; x.id === tileId)!;
+     if (t.type === 'property') {
+       const imp = propsState.byTileId[tileId]?.improvements ?? 0;
+       if (imp === 0) return null;
+       if (imp &gt;= 5) return &lt;span title="Hotel"&gt;🏨&lt;/span&gt;;
+       return (
+         &lt;span title={&#96;${imp} house${imp &gt; 1 ? 's' : ''}&#96;}&gt; {'🏠'.repeat(Math.min(4, imp))}&lt;/span&gt;
+       );
+     }
+     if (t.type === 'railroad') {
+       const owned = propsState.byTileId[tileId]?.ownerId;
+       return owned ? &lt;span title="Depot"&gt;🚉&lt;/span&gt; : null;
+     }
+     if (t.type === 'utility') {
+       const owned = propsState.byTileId[tileId]?.ownerId;
+       return owned ? &lt;span title="Utility"&gt;⚡&lt;/span&gt; : null;
+     }
+     return null;
+   };

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

Implemented the styled board tiles in the centered overlay:

- Colored header at the top matches property color group (railroad/utility have neutral headers).
- Bold title shows the tile name.
- Avatar row shows players currently on that tile (first 3, with +N if more).
- Footer:
  - Left: tile index.
  - Right: icon summary:
    - Property improvements: 🏠 (1–4) or 🏨 for hotel.
    - Railroad owned: 🚉
    - Utility owned: ⚡

Also added Use Bus Ticket:
- Button appears under the Roll section when player has tickets.
- Opens the centered board; selecting a tile consumes one ticket and shows a “✓ 🚐 to {Tile}” pill; tap to cancel.

-export function isMonopolyOwned(state: RootState, tileId: string, ownerId: string): boolean {

-  }
-  return true;
-}
+export function countGroupOwned(state: RootState, tileId: string, ownerId: string): { owned: number; total: number } {
+  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);
+  if (tile.type !== 'property' || !tile.group) return { owned: 0, total: 0 };
+  const ids = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =&gt; t.type === 'property' &amp;&amp; t.group === tile.group).map((t) =&gt; t.id);
+  let owned = 0;
+  for (const id of ids) {
+    const ps = state.properties.byTileId[id];
+    if (ps &amp;&amp; ps.ownerId === ownerId &amp;&amp; !ps.mortgaged) owned += 1;
+  }
+  return { owned, total: ids.length };
+}

- Properties now match your table for price and rents: site/partial/complete multipliers, 1–4 houses, hotel, skyscraper.

- Base rent doubles for partial set and triples for complete set (per your site/partial/complete columns).

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Implementing depot doubling**

I need to work on implementing depot doubling in rent, plus creating a UI that allows toggling depot ownership on railroads. Currently, the property slice has a `PropertyState` that includes `ownerId`, `mortgaged`, and `improvements`, but no depot flag. I’ll add a boolean depot flag specifically for railroads. 

The logic for the depot is that it doubles rent for each railroad owned. For now, I’ll focus on just the UI toggle without adjusting any money details since the user hasn’t provided depot costs.
**Updating the rent selector**

In the rent selector, if the tile is a railroad, I’ll compute the base rent based on the number of railroads owned. If `propsState.byTileId[tileId]?.depotInstalled` is true, I’ll multiply the rent by two. 

I also want to provide a UI update; in the PlayConsole under "gm-actions," we can add a "Depot Toggle" button when the current tile is a railroad. Additionally, I’ll create a reducer for `setDepotInstalled` and consider using an icon for the depot, like '🏗️' or '⛽', to indicate its presence. I’ll ensure these changes sync well!
**Implementing depot installed reducer**

I need to add the `setDepotInstalled` reducer, then update `rent.ts` to multiply rent by 2 if `depotInstalled` is true. Next, I’ll import `setDepotInstalled` into the PlayConsole and create an `onDepotToggle` handler, similar to how we did for mortgage actions. 

I'll place a "Toggle Depot" button alongside the existing mortgage buttons under GM actions. To dispatch the action, I can access `propsState.byTileId[tile.id]?.depotInstalled`. I’ll also consider logging an event for this toggle. Let’s move forward with the edits!</details></think>

+    else if (owned &gt;= 4) base = tile.railroad.rent4;
+    const depot = state.properties.byTileId[tileId]?.depotInstalled === true;
+    return depot ? base * 2 : base;

+                         {(() =&gt; {
+                           const tile = getTileByIndex(currentIndex);
+                           const pid = players[turnIndex]?.id || players[0]?.id;
+                           if (tile.type !== 'railroad' || !pid) return null;
+                           const ps = propsState.byTileId[tile.id];
+                           if (!ps || ps.ownerId !== pid) return null;
+                           const installed = ps.depotInstalled === true;
+                           const cost = 100;
+                           return (
+                             &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-2"&gt;
+                               {installed ? (
+                                 &lt;button
+                                   type="button"
+                                   className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-3 py-1.5 text-sm font-semibold border border-stone-500 text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-stone-50 dark:hover:bg-stone-900/30"
+                                   onClick={() =&gt; {
+                                     dispatch(setDepotInstalled({ tileId: tile.id, installed: false }));
+                                     dispatch(
+                                       appendEvent({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), gameId: 'local', type: 'MONEY_ADJUST', payload: { tileId: tile.id, message: 'Depot removed' }, createdAt: new Date().toISOString() })
+                                     );
+                                   }}
+                                 &gt;
+                                   Remove Depot
+                                 &lt;/button&gt;
+                               ) : (
+                                 &lt;button
+                                   type="button"
+                                   className={&#96;inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-md px-3 py-1.5 text-sm font-semibold border border-stone-700 text-stone-800 dark:text-stone-200 hover:bg-stone-50 dark:hover:bg-stone-900/30 ${
+                                     (players.find((x) =&gt; x.id === pid)?.money ?? 0) &gt;= cost ? '' : 'opacity-60 cursor-not-allowed'
+                                   }&#96;}
+                                   disabled={(players.find((x) =&gt; x.id === pid)?.money ?? 0) &lt; cost}
+                                   onClick={() =&gt; {
+                                     dispatch(setDepotInstalled({ tileId: tile.id, installed: true }));
+                                     dispatch(adjustPlayerMoney({ id: pid, delta: -cost }));
+                                     dispatch(
+                                       appendEvent({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), gameId: 'local', type: 'MONEY_ADJUST', payload: { tileId: tile.id, amount: -cost, message: 'Depot installed' }, moneyDelta: -cost, createdAt: new Date().toISOString() })
+                                     );
+                                   }}
+                                 &gt;
+                                   Install Depot ($100)
+                                 &lt;/button&gt;
+                               )}
+                             &lt;/div&gt;
+                           );
+                         })()}
                        &lt;/motion.div&gt;
                      )}
```

</details>

</tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

Fixed. Drawing a Bus was triggering the “turn change reset” effect because we listened to the whole players array (including ticket changes). I tightened the effect:

- Reset step state only when `turnIndex` changes.
- Track active player’s position separately; syncing `currentIndex` no longer resets the step.
- Bus draws now stay on the current step every time, and the ticket is added to inventory.

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

Implemented the rule:

- We snapshot `busTicketsAvailableThisTurn` at the start of the player’s turn.
- “Use Bus Ticket” shows only if that start-of-turn count > 0.
- Selecting a teleport consumes 1 ticket immediately and reduces the available count.
- Canceling the teleport restores the ticket to inventory and the available count.

+     grantGetOutOfJail(state, action: PayloadAction&lt;{ id: string; deck: 'chance' | 'community'; count?: number }&gt;) {
+       const p = state.players.find((x) =&gt; x.id === action.payload.id);
+       if (!p) return;
+       const n = action.payload.count ?? 1;
+       if (action.payload.deck === 'chance') p.gojfChance = Math.max(0, (p.gojfChance ?? 0) + n);
+       else p.gojfCommunity = Math.max(0, (p.gojfCommunity ?? 0) + n);
+     },
+     consumeGetOutOfJail(state, action: PayloadAction&lt;{ id: string; deck: 'chance' | 'community'; count?: number }&gt;) {

_**Agent (gpt-5)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file** • Edit file: /Users/pabloaguilar/Dropbox/code/monopolytracker/src/components/organisms/PlayConsole.tsx</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 20, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -516,4 +516,24 @@
      }
    }
+ 
+   function improvementLabel(imp: number): string {
+     if (imp &lt;= 0) return '—';
+     if (imp &gt;= 6) return 'Skyscraper';
+     if (imp === 5) return 'Hotel';
+     return &#96;H${imp}&#96;;
+   }
+ 
+   function groupTooltipForPlayer(playerId: string, group: ColorGroup): string {
+     const tiles = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =&gt; t.type === 'property' &amp;&amp; t.group === group);
+     const parts: string[] = [];
+     for (const t of tiles) {
+       const ps = propsState.byTileId[t.id];
+       const owned = ps?.ownerId === playerId;
+       if (!owned) continue;
+       const imp = ps?.improvements ?? 0;
+       parts.push(&#96;${t.name}: ${improvementLabel(imp)}&#96;);
+     }
+     return parts.length &gt; 0 ? parts.join(' • ') : '';
+   }

We need to focus on payment confirmation toggles for tax and fees, disabling End Turn until settled, adding a free parking pot, and adjusting the HUD.
**Outlining implementation details**

I could add properties for session slices, like a session.racePot and a session.freeParkingPot. The process would intercept when paying tax, as the current onPayTax dispatch adjusts money and events. I’d modify the session slice reducer to incorporate an addToFreeParking function and adjust the HUD to display the Free Parking pot. For depots, I need to show the remaining count based on installed railroads. Implementing some step gating is also necessary, with tracking for pending payments related to taxes.
**Implementing post-action logic**

So, post-actions come after movement, but payments should happen on landing. If predictedTo is set, I can call doQuickPayTax before onApplyMove, but they want the payment after the roll is confirmed and once the player lands. It sounds like a good plan to ensure any payment is executed upon End Turn, if payTaxSelected is true. I'll also add logic for the free parking pot and update the depot counter to show remaining units. Let's proceed with these minimal code changes!</details></think>

-  const doQuickPayTax = (pid: string): void =&gt; {
+  const doQuickPayTax = (pid: string): void =&gt; {
     const idx = predictedTo ?? currentIndex;
     const t = getTileByIndex(idx);
     if (t.type !== 'tax' || !t.taxAmount) return;
     dispatch(adjustPlayerMoney({ id: pid, delta: -t.taxAmount }));
     dispatch(
       appendEvent({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), gameId: 'local', type: 'FEE', payload: { tileId: t.id, from: pid, amount: t.taxAmount, message: &#96;Tax ${t.taxAmount}&#96; }, moneyDelta: -t.taxAmount, createdAt: new Date().toISOString() })
     );
+    dispatch(addToFreeParking(t.taxAmount));
   };
*** End Patch

  - If Special=Bus, End Turn stays disabled until a Bus card is selected.

- Free Parking pot: collects all tax/fee payments.
- HUD improvements:
  - Depots now show count installed; to show “depots left” we can set a total (e.g., 4) and display 4 − installed. Say the total you want and I’ll update it.

        "contents": "\n\nexport function computeRent(state: RootState, tileId: string, diceTotalForUtility = 0): number {\n  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);\n  const ps = state.properties.byTileId[tileId];\n  if (!ps || !ps.ownerId) return 0;\n  if (ps.mortgaged) return 0;\n\n  if (tile.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 tile.property) {\n    const imp = ps.improvements;\n    if (imp === 0) {\n      const base = tile.property.rent.base;\n      const { owned, total } = countGroupOwned(state, tileId, ps.ownerId);\n      if (owned \u003e= total \u0026\u0026 total \u003e 0) return base * 3; // complete set\n      if (owned \u003e= Math.ceil(total / 2)) return base * 2; // partial/half set\n      return base;\n    }\n    if (imp === 1) return tile.property.rent.house1;\n    if (imp === 2) return tile.property.rent.house2;\n    if (imp === 3) return tile.property.rent.house3;\n    if (imp === 4) return tile.property.rent.house4;\n    if (imp === 5) return tile.property.rent.hotel;\n    if (imp \u003e= 6) return tile.property.rent.skyscraper ?? tile.property.rent.hotel;\n    return tile.property.rent.base;\n  }\n\n  if (tile.type === 'railroad' \u0026\u0026 tile.railroad) {\n    const owned = countRailroadsOwned(state, ps.ownerId);\n    let base = tile.railroad.rent1;\n    if (owned === 2) base = tile.railroad.rent2;\n    else if (owned === 3) base = tile.railroad.rent3;\n    else if (owned \u003e= 4) base = tile.railroad.rent4;\n    const depot = state.properties.byTileId[tileId]?.depotInstalled === true;\n    return depot ? base * 2 : base;\n  }\n\n  if (tile.type === 'utility' \u0026\u0026 tile.utility) {\n    const owned = countUtilitiesOwned(state, ps.ownerId);\n    if (owned \u003e= 3 \u0026\u0026 (tile.utility as any).rentMultiplier3) {\n      return diceTotalForUtility * (tile.utility as any).rentMultiplier3;\n    }\n    const mult = owned \u003e= 2 ? tile.utility.rentMultiplier2 : tile.utility.rentMultiplier1;\n    return diceTotalForUtility * mult;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}",

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport function computeRent(state: RootState, tileId: string, diceTotalForUtility = 0): number {\n  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);\n  const ps = state.properties.byTileId[tileId];\n  if (!ps || !ps.ownerId) return 0;\n  if (ps.mortgaged) return 0;\n\n  if (tile.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 tile.property) {\n    const imp = ps.improvements;\n    if (imp === 0) {\n      const base = tile.property.rent.base;\n      const { owned, total } = countGroupOwned(state, tileId, ps.ownerId);\n      if (owned \u003e= total \u0026\u0026 total \u003e 0) return base * 3; // complete set\n      if (owned \u003e= Math.ceil(total / 2)) return base * 2; // partial/half set\n      return base;\n    }\n    if (imp === 1) return tile.property.rent.house1;\n    if (imp === 2) return tile.property.rent.house2;\n    if (imp === 3) return tile.property.rent.house3;\n    if (imp === 4) return tile.property.rent.house4;\n    if (imp === 5) return tile.property.rent.hotel;\n    if (imp \u003e= 6) return tile.property.rent.skyscraper ?? tile.property.rent.hotel;\n    return tile.property.rent.base;\n  }\n\n  if (tile.type === 'railroad' \u0026\u0026 tile.railroad) {\n    const owned = countRailroadsOwned(state, ps.ownerId);\n    let base = tile.railroad.rent1;\n    if (owned === 2) base = tile.railroad.rent2;\n    else if (owned === 3) base = tile.railroad.rent3;\n    else if (owned \u003e= 4) base = tile.railroad.rent4;\n    const depot = state.properties.byTileId[tileId]?.depotInstalled === true;\n    return depot ? base * 2 : base;\n  }\n\n  if (tile.type === 'utility' \u0026\u0026 tile.utility) {\n    const owned = countUtilitiesOwned(state, ps.ownerId);\n    if (owned \u003e= 3 \u0026\u0026 (tile.utility as any).rentMultiplier3) {\n      return diceTotalForUtility * (tile.utility as any).rentMultiplier3;\n    }\n    const mult = owned \u003e= 2 ? tile.utility.rentMultiplier2 : tile.utility.rentMultiplier1;\n    return diceTotalForUtility * mult;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}",

        "contents": "export default function PlayConsole(): JSX.Element {\n\n\n  ...\n\n\n  // Reset confirmation if any die changes\n  React.useEffect(() =\u003e {\n    setRollConfirmed(false);\n  }, [d6A, d6B, special]);\n\n  // //#derived\n  const lastChance = cardsState.decks.chance.discardPile[0];\n  const lastCommunity = cardsState.decks.community.discardPile[0];\n  const lastBus = cardsState.decks.bus.discardPile[0];\n\n  const specialNumeric: number = useMemo(() =\u003e {\n    if (special === '+1') return 1;\n    if (special === '-1') return -1;\n    if (typeof special === 'number') return special;\n    // Treat Bus (string) as 0 steps\n    return 0;\n  }, [special]);\n\n  const hasRoll = d6A !== null \u0026\u0026 d6B !== null; \n  const hasFullNumericRoll = d6A !== null \u0026\u0026 d6B !== null \u0026\u0026 typeof special === 'number';\n  const hasRollWithSpecialSelected = d6A !== null \u0026\u0026 d6B !== null \u0026\u0026 special !== null; // includes Bus\n  const requiresBusCard = special === 'Bus';\n  const rollTotal = (d6A ?? 0) + (d6B ?? 0) + specialNumeric;\n\n  const rollForEval: DieRoll | null = useMemo(() =\u003e {\n    if (!hasRoll) return null;\n    const sp: SpecialDieFace = (special ?? 1) as SpecialDieFace; // fallback only for typing\n    return {\n      d6A: d6A as number,\n      d6B: d6B as number,\n      special: sp,\n      isDouble: (d6A as number) === (d6B as number),\n      isTriple: (d6A as number) === (d6B as number) \u0026\u0026 (typeof sp === 'number' ? sp === (d6A as number) : false),\n      isTripleOnes: (d6A as number) === 1 \u0026\u0026 (d6B as number) === 1 \u0026\u0026 sp === 1,\n    };\n  }, [d6A, d6B, special, hasRoll]);\n\n  const bankUnownedCount = useMemo(() =\u003e {\n    return Object.values(propsState.byTileId).filter((ps) =\u003e ps \u0026\u0026 ps.ownerId === null).length;\n  }, [propsState.byTileId]);\n\n  const depotsInstalled = useMemo(() =\u003e {\n    return BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'railroad').reduce((acc, t) =\u003e (propsState.byTileId[t.id]?.depotInstalled ? acc + 1 : acc), 0);\n  }, [propsState.byTileId]);",

0) + (d6B ?? 0) + specialNumeric;\n\n  const rollForEval: DieRoll | null = useMemo(() =\u003e {\n    if (!hasRoll) return null;\n    const sp: SpecialDieFace = (special ?? 1) as SpecialDieFace; // fallback only for typing\n    return {\n      d6A: d6A as number,\n      d6B: d6B as number,\n      special: sp,\n      isDouble: (d6A as number) === (d6B as number),\n      isTriple: (d6A as number) === (d6B as number) \u0026\u0026 (typeof sp === 'number' ?         "originalContents": "\n\nexport interface PropertyState {\n  ownerId: OwnerId;\n  mortgaged: boolean;\n  improvements: number; // 0..4 houses, 5 = hotel, 6 = skyscraper\n  depotInstalled?: boolean; // for railroads: doubles rent\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nconst initialState: PropertiesState = {\n  byTileId: Object.fromEntries(\n    BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' || t.type === 'railroad' || t.type === 'utility').map((t) =\u003e [t.id, { ownerId: null, mortgaged: false, improvements: 0, depotInstalled: false }])\n  ),\n  housesRemaining: 32,\n  hotelsRemaining: 12,\n};\n\nfunction getGroupTileIds(tileId: string): string[] {\n  const tile = BOARD_TILES.find((t) =\u003e t.id === tileId);\n  if (!tile) return [];\n  if (tile.type !== 'property' || !tile.group) return [tileId];\n  return BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 t.group === tile.group).map((t) =\u003e t.id);\n}",

        "originalContents": "\n\nconst initialState: PropertiesState = {\n  byTileId: Object.fromEntries(\n    BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' || t.type === 'railroad' || t.type === 'utility').map((t) =\u003e [t.id, { ownerId: null, mortgaged: false, improvements: 0, depotInstalled: false }])\n  ),\n  housesRemaining: 32,\n  hotelsRemaining: 12,\n};\n\nfunction getGroupTileIds(tileId: string): string[] {\n  const tile = BOARD_TILES.find((t) =\u003e t.id === tileId);\n  if (!tile) return [];\n  if (tile.type !== 'property' || !tile.group) return [tileId];\n  return BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 t.group === tile.group).map((t) =\u003e t.id);\n}",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport function countRailroadsOwned(state: RootState, ownerId: string): number {\n  return BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'railroad').reduce((acc, t) =\u003e (state.properties.byTileId[t.id]?.ownerId === ownerId ? acc + 1 : acc), 0);\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n//#data\n// Note: Economics are approximations based on classic values with modest scaling\n// to provide functional rents. Can be tuned later to exact Winning Moves schedule.\nexport const BOARD_TILES: BoardTileData[] = [\n  { id: 'go', index: 0, name: 'GO', type: 'go' },\n  // Brown set\n  { id: 'mediterranean-ave', index: 1, name: 'Mediterranean Avenue', type: 'property', group: 'brown', property: { purchasePrice: 60, mortgageValue: 30, houseCost: 50, hotelCost: 50, skyscraperCost: 100, rent: { base: 2, house1: 10, house2: 30, house3: 90, house4: 160, hotel: 250, skyscraper: 750 } } },\n  { id: 'community-1', index: 2, name: 'Community Chest', type: 'community' },\n  { id: 'baltic-ave', index: 3, name: 'Baltic Avenue', type: 'property', group: 'brown', property: { purchasePrice: 60, mortgageValue: 30, houseCost: 50, hotelCost: 50, skyscraperCost: 100, rent: { base: 4, house1: 20, house2: 60, house3: 180, house4: 320, hotel: 450, skyscraper: 950 } } },\n  { id: 'arctic-ave', index: 4, name: 'Arctic Avenue', type: 'property', group: 'brown', property: { purchasePrice: 80, mortgageValue: 40, houseCost: 50, hotelCost: 50, skyscraperCost: 100, rent: { base: 5, house1: 30, house2: 80, house3: 240, house4: 360, hotel: 500, skyscraper: 950 } } },\n  { id: 'income-tax', index: 5, name: 'Income Tax', type: 'tax', taxAmount: 200 },\n  { id: 'reading-railroad', index: 6, name: 'Reading Railroad', type: 'railroad', railroad: { purchasePrice: 200, mortgageValue: 100, rent1: 25, rent2: 50, rent3: 100, rent4: 200, hasDepot: true } },\n  // Light Blue set\n  { id: 'massachusetts-ave', index: 7, name: 'Massachusetts Avenue', type: 'property', group: 'lightBlue', property: { purchasePrice: 100, mortgageValue: 50, houseCost: 50, hotelCost: 50, skyscraperCost: 100, rent: { base: 6, house1: 30, house2: 90, house3: 270, house4: 400, hotel: 550, skyscraper: 1050 } } },",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "export const GO_TO_JAIL_INDEX = 39;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88
          },

        "originalContents": ",\n    setDepotInstalled(state, action: PayloadAction\u003c{ tileId: string; installed: boolean }\u003e) {\n      const ps = state.byTileId[action.payload.tileId];\n      if (!ps) return;\n      const tile = BOARD_TILES.find((t) =\u003e t.id === action.payload.tileId);\n      if (!tile || tile.type !== 'railroad') return;\n      ps.depotInstalled = action.payload.installed;\n    }",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nconst initialState: SessionState = {\n  currentGameId: null,\n  racePot: defaultRacePot(),\n  turnIndex: 0,\n  freeParkingPot: 0,\n};\n\nconst sessionSlice = createSlice({\n  name: 'session',\n  initialState,\n  reducers: {\n    setCurrentGameId(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cstring | null\u003e) {\n      state.currentGameId = action.payload;\n    },\n    initializeRacePot(state, action: PayloadAction\u003c{ participants: string[]; amountPerPlayer: number }\u003e) {\n      // Backfill for persisted sessions that predate racePot\n      if (!state.racePot) state.racePot = defaultRacePot();\n      const { participants, amountPerPlayer } = action.payload;\n      if (participants.length \u003c 2 || state.racePot.active) return;\n      state.racePot.active = true;\n      state.racePot.participants = participants;\n      state.racePot.amount = participants.length * amountPerPlayer;\n      state.racePot.winnerId = null;\n    },\n    resetRacePot(state) {\n      state.racePot = defaultRacePot();\n    },\n    setRacePotWinner(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cstring\u003e) {\n      if (!state.racePot) state.racePot = defaultRacePot();\n      if (!state.racePot.active || state.racePot.winnerId) return;\n      state.racePot.winnerId = action.payload;\n    },\n    setTurnIndex(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cnumber\u003e) {\n      state.turnIndex = Math.max(0, action.payload);\n    },\n    advanceTurn(state, action: PayloadAction\u003c{ playerCount: number }\u003e) {\n      const n = action.payload.playerCount;\n      if (n \u003c= 0) {\n        state.turnIndex = 0;\n      } else {\n        state.turnIndex = (state.turnIndex + 1) % n;\n      }\n    },\n    addToFreeParking(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cnumber\u003e) {\n      state.freeParkingPot += action.payload;\n      if (state.freeParkingPot \u003c 0) state.freeParkingPot = 0;\n    },\n    resetFreeParking(state) {\n      state.freeParkingPot = 0;\n    },\n  },\n});\n\nexport const { setCurrentGameId, initializeRacePot, resetRacePot, setRacePotWinner, setTurnIndex, advanceTurn, addToFreeParking, resetFreeParking } = sessionSlice.actions;",

        "originalContents": "\n\nconst initialState: SessionState = {\n  currentGameId: null,\n  racePot: defaultRacePot(),\n  turnIndex: 0,\n  freeParkingPot: 0,\n};\n\nconst sessionSlice = createSlice({\n  name: 'session',\n  initialState,\n  reducers: {\n    setCurrentGameId(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cstring | null\u003e) {\n      state.currentGameId = action.payload;\n    },\n    initializeRacePot(state, action: PayloadAction\u003c{ participants: string[]; amountPerPlayer: number }\u003e) {\n      // Backfill for persisted sessions that predate racePot\n      if (!state.racePot) state.racePot = defaultRacePot();\n      const { participants, amountPerPlayer } = action.payload;\n      if (participants.length \u003c 2 || state.racePot.active) return;\n      state.racePot.active = true;\n      state.racePot.participants = participants;\n      state.racePot.amount = participants.length * amountPerPlayer;\n      state.racePot.winnerId = null;\n    },\n    resetRacePot(state) {\n      state.racePot = defaultRacePot();\n    },\n    setRacePotWinner(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cstring\u003e) {\n      if (!state.racePot) state.racePot = defaultRacePot();\n      if (!state.racePot.active || state.racePot.winnerId) return;\n      state.racePot.winnerId = action.payload;\n    },\n    setTurnIndex(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cnumber\u003e) {\n      state.turnIndex = Math.max(0, action.payload);\n    },\n    advanceTurn(state, action: PayloadAction\u003c{ playerCount: number }\u003e) {\n      const n = action.payload.playerCount;\n      if (n \u003c= 0) {\n        state.turnIndex = 0;\n      } else {\n        state.turnIndex = (state.turnIndex + 1) % n;\n      }\n    },\n    addToFreeParking(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cnumber\u003e) {\n      state.freeParkingPot += action.payload;\n      if (state.freeParkingPot \u003c 0) state.freeParkingPot = 0;\n    },\n    resetFreeParking(state) {\n      state.freeParkingPot = 0;\n    },\n  },\n});\n\nexport const { setCurrentGameId, initializeRacePot, resetRacePot, setRacePotWinner, setTurnIndex, advanceTurn, addToFreeParking, resetFreeParking } = sessionSlice.actions;",

        "originalContents": "\n  { id: 'electric-company', index: 17, name: 'Electric Company', type: 'utility', utility: { purchasePrice: 150, mortgageValue: 75, rentMultiplier1: 4, rentMultiplier2: 10, rentMultiplier3: 20 } },\n  { id: 'states-ave', index: 18, name: 'States Avenue', type: 'property', group: 'pink', property: { purchasePrice: 140, mortgageValue: 70, houseCost: 100, hotelCost: 100, skyscraperCost: 200, rent: { base: 10, house1: 50, house2: 150, house3: 450, house4: 625, hotel: 750, skyscraper: 1250 } } },\n  { id: 'virginia-ave', index: 19, name: 'Virginia Avenue', type: 'property', group: 'pink', property: { purchasePrice: 160, mortgageValue: 80, houseCost: 100, hotelCost: 100, skyscraperCost: 200, rent: { base: 12, house1: 60, house2: 180, house3: 500, house4: 700, hotel: 900, skyscraper: 1400 } } },\n  { id: 'pennsylvania-railroad', index: 20, name: 'Pennsylvania Railroad', type: 'railroad', railroad: { purchasePrice: 200, mortgageValue: 100, rent1: 25, rent2: 50, rent3: 100, rent4: 200, hasDepot: true } },\n  // Orange set\n  { id: 'st-james-place', index: 21, name: 'St. James Place', type: 'property', group: 'orange', property: { purchasePrice: 180, mortgageValue: 90, houseCost: 100, hotelCost: 100, skyscraperCost: 200, rent: { base: 14, house1: 70, house2: 200, house3: 550, house4: 750, hotel: 950, skyscraper: 1450 } } },\n  { id: 'community-2', index: 22, name: 'Community Chest', type: 'community' },\n  { id: 'tennessee-ave', index: 23, name: 'Tennessee Avenue', type: 'property', group: 'orange', property: { purchasePrice: 180, mortgageValue: 90, houseCost: 100, hotelCost: 100, skyscraperCost: 200, rent: { base: 14, house1: 70, house2: 200, house3: 550, house4: 750, hotel: 950, skyscraper: 1450 } } },",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport function countGroupOwned(state: RootState, tileId: string, ownerId: string): { owned: number; total: number } {\n  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);\n  if (tile.type !== 'property' || !tile.group) return { owned: 0, total: 0 };\n  const ids = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 t.group === tile.group).map((t) =\u003e t.id);\n  let owned = 0;\n  for (const id of ids) {\n    const ps = state.properties.byTileId[id];\n    if (ps \u0026\u0026 ps.ownerId === ownerId \u0026\u0026 !ps.mortgaged) owned += 1;\n  }\n  return { owned, total: ids.length };\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "export function getBoardTile(tileId: string) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "  if (!t) throw new Error(&#96;Unknown tile: ${tileId}&#96;);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "  return t;"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "export function countGroupOwned(state: RootState, tileId: string, ownerId: string): { owned: number; total: number } {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "  if (tile.type !== 'property' || !tile.group) return { owned: 0, total: 0 };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "  const ids = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 t.group === tile.group).map((t) =\u003e t.id);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "  let owned = 0;"

          {
            "lineNumber": 21,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    const ps = state.properties.byTileId[id];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    if (ps \u0026\u0026 ps.ownerId === ownerId \u0026\u0026 !ps.mortgaged) owned += 1;"
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,

          },

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\nexport function countGroupOwned(state: RootState, tileId: string, ownerId: string): { owned: number; total: number } {\n  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);\n  if (tile.type !== 'property' || !tile.group) return { owned: 0, total: 0 };\n  const ids = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 t.group === tile.group).map((t) =\u003e t.id);\n  let owned = 0;\n  for (const id of ids) {\n    const ps = state.properties.byTileId[id];\n    if (ps \u0026\u0026 ps.ownerId === ownerId \u0026\u0026 !ps.mortgaged) owned += 1;\n  }\n  return { owned, total: ids.length };\n}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 29
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 9
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/features/selectors/rent.ts"
      },
      "score": 0.2001952975988388
    }
  ]
}

        "originalContents": "// #index\n// - //#imports: hooks and selectors for spectator data\n// - //#render: spectator dashboard (players, bank, unowned, most landed, groups)\n\n// //#imports\nimport React from 'react';\nimport { useSelector } from 'react-redux';\nimport { selectSpectatorSummary, selectBankBuildingCounts, selectPlayerPropertyDetails, selectUnownedProperties, selectColorGroupOwnership, selectMostLandedTile } from '@/features/selectors/stats';\n\nexport default function SpectatorDashboard(): JSX.Element {\n  const players = useSelector(selectSpectatorSummary);\n  const playerDetails = useSelector(selectPlayerPropertyDetails);\n  const bank = useSelector(selectBankBuildingCounts);\n  const unowned = useSelector(selectUnownedProperties);\n  const groupOwnership = useSelector(selectColorGroupOwnership);\n  const mostLanded = useSelector(selectMostLandedTile);",

        "originalContents": "import { createSelector } from '@reduxjs/toolkit';\nimport type { RootState } from '@/app/store';\nimport { BOARD_TILES } from '@/data/board';\n\nexport const selectPlayers = (state: RootState) =\u003e state.players.players;",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport function computeRent(state: RootState, tileId: string, diceTotalForUtility = 0): number {\n  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);\n  const ps = state.properties.byTileId[tileId];\n  if (!ps || !ps.ownerId) return 0;\n  if (ps.mortgaged) return 0;\n\n  if (tile.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 tile.property) {\n    const imp = ps.improvements;\n    if (imp === 0) {\n      const base = tile.property.rent.base;\n      const { owned, total } = countGroupOwned(state, tileId, ps.ownerId);\n      if (owned \u003e= total \u0026\u0026 total \u003e 0) return base * 3; // complete set\n      if (owned \u003e= Math.ceil(total / 2)) return base * 2; // partial/half set\n      return base;\n    }\n    if (imp === 1) return tile.property.rent.house1;\n    if (imp === 2) return tile.property.rent.house2;\n    if (imp === 3) return tile.property.rent.house3;\n    if (imp === 4) return tile.property.rent.house4;\n    if (imp === 5) return tile.property.rent.hotel;\n    if (imp \u003e= 6) return tile.property.rent.skyscraper ?? tile.property.rent.hotel;\n    return tile.property.rent.base;\n  }\n\n  if (tile.type === 'railroad' \u0026\u0026 tile.railroad) {\n    const owned = countRailroadsOwned(state, ps.ownerId);\n    let base = tile.railroad.rent1;\n    if (owned === 2) base = tile.railroad.rent2;\n    else if (owned === 3) base = tile.railroad.rent3;\n    else if (owned \u003e= 4) base = tile.railroad.rent4;\n    const depot = state.properties.byTileId[tileId]?.depotInstalled === true;\n    return depot ? base * 2 : base;\n  }\n\n  if (tile.type === 'utility' \u0026\u0026 tile.utility) {\n    const owned = countUtilitiesOwned(state, ps.ownerId);\n    if (owned \u003e= 3 \u0026\u0026 (tile.utility as any).rentMultiplier3) {\n      return diceTotalForUtility * (tile.utility as any).rentMultiplier3;\n    }\n    const mult = owned \u003e= 2 ? tile.utility.rentMultiplier2 : tile.utility.rentMultiplier1;\n    return diceTotalForUtility * mult;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\nexport function computeRent(state: RootState, tileId: string, diceTotalForUtility = 0): number {\n  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);\n  const ps = state.properties.byTileId[tileId];\n  if (!ps || !ps.ownerId) return 0;\n  if (ps.mortgaged) return 0;\n\n  if (tile.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 tile.property) {\n    const imp = ps.improvements;\n    if (imp === 0) {\n      const base = tile.property.rent.base;\n      const { owned, total } = countGroupOwned(state, tileId, ps.ownerId);\n      if (owned \u003e= total \u0026\u0026 total \u003e 0) return base * 3; // complete set\n      if (owned \u003e= Math.ceil(total / 2)) return base * 2; // partial/half set\n      return base;\n    }\n    if (imp === 1) return tile.property.rent.house1;\n    if (imp === 2) return tile.property.rent.house2;\n    if (imp === 3) return tile.property.rent.house3;\n    if (imp === 4) return tile.property.rent.house4;\n    if (imp === 5) return tile.property.rent.hotel;\n    if (imp \u003e= 6) return tile.property.rent.skyscraper ?? tile.property.rent.hotel;\n    return tile.property.rent.base;\n  }\n\n  if (tile.type === 'railroad' \u0026\u0026 tile.railroad) {\n    const owned = countRailroadsOwned(state, ps.ownerId);\n    let base = tile.railroad.rent1;\n    if (owned === 2) base = tile.railroad.rent2;\n    else if (owned === 3) base = tile.railroad.rent3;\n    else if (owned \u003e= 4) base = tile.railroad.rent4;\n    const depot = state.properties.byTileId[tileId]?.depotInstalled === true;\n    return depot ? base * 2 : base;\n  }\n\n  if (tile.type === 'utility' \u0026\u0026 tile.utility) {\n    const owned = countUtilitiesOwned(state, ps.ownerId);\n    if (owned \u003e= 3 \u0026\u0026 (tile.utility as any).rentMultiplier3) {\n      return diceTotalForUtility * (tile.utility as any).rentMultiplier3;\n    }\n    const mult = owned \u003e= 2 ? tile.utility.rentMultiplier2 : tile.utility.rentMultiplier1;\n    return diceTotalForUtility * mult;\n  }\n\n  return 0;\n}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 79
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 31
          }

        "originalContents": "\n\nexport const selectMostLandedProperty = createSelector(selectEvents, () =\u003e {\n  const counts: Record\u003cstring, number\u003e = {};\n  for (const ev of (selectEvents as any).source?.events ?? []) {\n    // placeholder\n  }\n  return null;\n});",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport const selectMostLandedTile = createSelector(selectEvents, (events) =\u003e {\n  const counts: Record\u003cstring, number\u003e = {};\n  for (const ev of events) {\n    if (ev.type === 'MOVE' \u0026\u0026 typeof ev.payload?.to === 'number') {\n      const tile = BOARD_TILES[ev.payload.to];\n      if (tile \u0026\u0026 tile.type === 'property') counts[tile.id] = (counts[tile.id] || 0) + 1;\n    }\n  }\n  let topId: string | null = null;\n  let topCount = 0;\n  for (const [id, c] of Object.entries(counts)) {\n    if (c \u003e topCount) {\n      topCount = c;\n      topId = id;\n    }\n  }\n  if (!topId) return null;\n  const t = BOARD_TILES.find((x) =\u003e x.id === topId);\n  return t ? { id: topId, name: t.name, count: topCount } : null;\n});",

        "contents": "\n\nexport const selectColorGroupOwnership = createSelector(selectProperties, () =\u003e {\n  const groups: Record\u003cstring, { total: number; owners: Record\u003cstring, number\u003e }\u003e = {};\n  for (const tile of BOARD_TILES) {\n    if (tile.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 tile.group) {\n      if (!groups[tile.group]) groups[tile.group] = { total: 0, owners: {} };\n      groups[tile.group].total += 1;\n      const ownerId = (window as any).__store__?.getState?.()?.properties?.byTileId?.[tile.id]?.ownerId as string | null;\n      if (ownerId) {\n        groups[tile.group].owners[ownerId] = (groups[tile.group].owners[ownerId] || 0) + 1;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return groups;\n});",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nexport function countGroupOwned(state: RootState, tileId: string, ownerId: string): { owned: number; total: number } {\n  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);\n  if (tile.type !== 'property' || !tile.group) return { owned: 0, total: 0 };\n  const ids = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 t.group === tile.group).map((t) =\u003e t.id);\n  let owned = 0;\n  for (const id of ids) {\n    const ps = state.properties.byTileId[id];\n    if (ps \u0026\u0026 ps.ownerId === ownerId \u0026\u0026 !ps.mortgaged) owned += 1;\n  }\n  return { owned, total: ids.length };\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\nexport function countGroupOwned(state: RootState, tileId: string, ownerId: string): { owned: number; total: number } {\n  const tile = getBoardTile(tileId);\n  if (tile.type !== 'property' || !tile.group) return { owned: 0, total: 0 };\n  const ids = BOARD_TILES.filter((t) =\u003e t.type === 'property' \u0026\u0026 t.group === tile.group).map((t) =\u003e t.id);\n  let owned = 0;\n  for (const id of ids) {\n    const ps = state.properties.byTileId[id];\n    if (ps \u0026\u0026 ps.ownerId === ownerId \u0026\u0026 !ps.mortgaged) owned += 1;\n  }\n  return { owned, total: ids.length };\n}",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 29
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 9
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\nconst initialState: CardsState = {\n  seed: DEFAULT_SEED,\n  decks: {\n    chance: initDeck(DEFAULT_SEED, 'chance'),\n    community: initDeck(DEFAULT_SEED, 'community'),\n    bus: initDeck(DEFAULT_SEED, 'bus'),\n  },\n};\n\nconst cardsSlice = createSlice({\n  name: 'cards',\n  initialState,\n  reducers: {\n    setSeed(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cnumber | string\u003e) {\n      state.seed = action.payload;\n      state.decks.chance = initDeck(state.seed, 'chance');\n      state.decks.community = initDeck(state.seed, 'community');\n      state.decks.bus = initDeck(state.seed, 'bus');\n    },\n    drawCard(state, action: PayloadAction\u003cCardDeck\u003e) {\n      const deckKey = action.payload;\n      const deck = state.decks[deckKey];\n      if (deck.drawPile.length === 0) {\n        const reshuffled = buildShuffledDeck(deckKey, &#96;${state.seed}:${deckKey}:r${deck.discardPile.length}&#96;);\n        deck.drawPile = reshuffled;\n        deck.discardPile = [];\n      }\n      const card = deck.drawPile.shift();\n      if (card) {\n        deck.discardPile.unshift(card);\n      }\n    },\n    putCardOnBottom(state, action: PayloadAction\u003c{ deck: CardDeck; cardId: string }\u003e) {\n      const { deck: deckKey, cardId } = action.payload;\n      const deck = state.decks[deckKey];\n      const idx = deck.discardPile.findIndex((c) =\u003e c.id === cardId);\n      if (idx \u003e= 0) {\n        const [card] = deck.discardPile.splice(idx, 1);\n        deck.drawPile.push(card);\n      }\n    },\n  },\n});\n\nexport const selectLastDrawnCard = (state: CardsState, deck: CardDeck): CardDefinition | null =\u003e {\n  const d = state.decks[deck];\n  return d.discardPile.length \u003e 0 ? d.discardPile[0] : null;\n};",
